title: Conformance case metadata
description: The shape of a conformance case's case.json: machine-readable metadata about the case, kept beside the definition rather than inside it so the definition stays a pristine example workflow. Defined for this suite.

description.description: A one-line statement of the pattern the case demonstrates.

profiles.description: The conformance claim the case requires, as the profile URIs defined in the reference's Conformance appendix: the full claim, not a delta. Every case includes the Core URI, since a conformance claim always includes Core; an expression case adds the CEL profile, and a case relying on a CEL extension would add that extension's profile. A runner selects the case by matching these URIs, character for character, against the implementation's conformance claim.

marks.description: Runner-facing execution marks, distinct from conformance profiles: traits that affect how a runner executes the case, not which profiles an implementation must claim. temporal marks a case whose outcome depends on real elapsed time, so the run takes wall-clock time. An empty array means no marks apply.

scenarios.description: The case's scenarios, keyed by name: each is one execution of the definition, with its data files in the scenarios/<name>/ directory of the same name. A case with a single scenario conventionally names it base.

description.description: A one-line statement of what the scenario's inputs probe. Omitted when the case description already covers it, as in a single-scenario case.
